question:
Will the price of Bitcoin be between $116,000 and $118,000 on October 19?

Description: This market will resolve according to the final "Close" price of the Binance 1 minute candle for BTC/USDT 12:00 in the ET timezone (noon) on the date specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the BTC/USDT "Close" prices currently available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/BTC_USDT with "1m" and "Candles" selected on the top bar.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

Please note that this market is about the price according to Binance BTC/USDT, not according to other sources or spot markets.

evidences.title: Fed Meeting 2025–2027 and the Crypto Market
evidences.content: The next Federal Reserve meeting is on October 28–29 2025, with markets pricing in a likely rate cut of 25 bps. No meeting occurs in November. These decisions directly affect crypto liquidity, DeFi yields, and investor risk appetite worldwide. ## Key Takeaways ## Federal Reserve Meeting Schedule ### 2025 Calendar ## 🗓 2025 Federal Reserve (FOMC) Meeting Calendar [...] ## 🗓 2025 Federal Reserve (FOMC) Meeting Calendar | Month | Dates | Key Info | --- | January | 28 – 29 | Regular meeting | | March \ | 18 – 19 | Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) | | May | 6 – 7 | Regular meeting | | June \ | 17 – 18 | SEP + Press Conference | | July | 29 – 30 | Regular meeting | | August | 22 | Notation Vote | | September \ | 16 – 17 | SEP + Press Conference | | October | 28 – 29 | Next meeting – possible rate cut | | December \ | 9 – 10 | Final meeting of the year + SEP | Month Dates Key Info January 28 – 29 Regular meeting March \ 18 – 19 Summary of Economic Projections (SEP) May 6 – 7 Regular meeting June \ 17 – 18 SEP + Press Conference July 29 – 30 Regular meeting August 22 Notation Vote September \ 16 – 17 SEP + Press Conference [...] December \ 8 – 9 Final meeting of 2027 + SEP \ Meetin...

evidences.title: Bitcoin Peak Coming in October or November, “Fragile Stabilization ...
evidences.content: The late-cycle signal usually appears when the market overheats relative to the long-term holder base. Based on the current trajectory, Adler estimated that this signal could appear between October and November 2025, provided it coincides with a wave of experienced holder selling and a spike in volatility that eventually begins to fade. ## Short-Term Structure Is Fragile Glassnode data shows that Bitcoin's short-term momentum remains fragile. Over the past week, the asset has been trading just above the short-term holder cost basis, hovering around $111,000. Indicators showed a partial recovery as RSI climbed toward neutral territory and Spot CVD improved, but trading volumes fell, reflecting a lack of conviction.

evidences.title: Flows Are Back: Bitcoin, ETFs, and the Fed in Focus
evidences.content: ### NEWSLETTER 8 - 15 Sep 2025 “Flows are back.” BTC/ETH ETFs posted one of the strongest weekly nets since July as softer inflation and labor data tilted the Fed firmly toward easing. A surprise contraction in PPI and higher jobless claims gave the market the green light, sending Bitcoin to $116k and igniting flows into ETFs and high-beta alts. Risk appetite returned selectively (SOL, Toncoin), while corporate treasuries and DeFi protocols deepened their exposure to U.S. Treasuries. With the ECB holding steady and the Fed now in focus, markets are primed for a cut this week. ### Price Action & Flows Bitcoin advanced steadily, climbing from $111k to $116k, briefly testing all-time-high levels before consolidating. The move was underpinned by heavy ETF demand: [...] For crypto, the test is whether ETF inflows can sustain above $2B per week after the Fed. Solana’s ETF growth and Toncoin’s momentum remain in focus, alongside tokenized Treasury adoption by banks and asset managers. In traditional markets, dollar and U.S. Treasury moves will dictate whether risk appetite holds. On the regulatory front, the SEC’s October 17 roundtable and the BoE’s consultation feedback loom, while Mi...

evidences.title: Can Bitcoin's Price Hit $150,000 In 2025? Analyzing The Outlook ...
evidences.content: ## Can Bitcoin Hit $150,000 In 2025? The path of least resistance is higher—supply growth is now below 0.8% annually post-halving, while demand from pensions, sovereign funds and retail apps keeps climbing. If ETF net inflows repeat Q1’s average $280 million per day, the modelled stock-to-flow ratio tips toward $175,000 by next summer. Yet timing matters. The last two cycles saw 30-40% mid-cycle draw-downs before blowing off. A correction to the low-$90s in late-2025, followed by a Q4 surge, could still land BTC above $150,000 by New Year’s Eve. In other words, yes—but probably not in a straight line. [...] Digital Assets News Crypto Prices NFT Prices Learn Forbes Digital Assets # Can Bitcoin’s Price Hit $150,000 In 2025? Analyzing The Outlook For BTC ByZennon Kapron, Forbes contributors publish independent expert analyses and insights. I write about how fintech is disrupting the financial industry. Could Bitcoin hit $150k in 2025? (Photo by Chris McGrath/Getty Images) Getty Images Bitcoin is once again center stage. After breaching six figures for the first time in March and notching an all-time high near $112,000 in May, the world’s largest digital asset has slipped into a tig...

evidences.title: IBIT ETF: BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust Explained
evidences.content: Understanding the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF, comparing IBIT vs FBTC, evaluating the IBIT expense ratio, and determining whether IBIT represents the best Bitcoin ETF requires examining the structure, scale, and capital flows driving the fund. ## iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF Overview The iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) is a spot Bitcoin exchange-traded fund that holds actual Bitcoin as its underlying asset. The trust itself was founded on June 8, 2023, with BlackRock establishing the structure ahead of anticipated regulatory approval for spot Bitcoin ETFs. The fund later launched for public trading on January 5, 2024. [...] Spot Bitcoin ETFs have fundamentally changed how traditional capital accesses cryptocurrency markets. Instead of buying Bitcoin directly through crypto exchanges or managing digital wallets, investors can gain exposure through regulated exchange-traded funds listed on major stock exchanges. Among these funds, the iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF (IBIT) has rapidly emerged as one of the most dominant vehicles for institutional and retail Bitcoin exposure. Issued by BlackRock, the world’s largest asset manager, IBIT has accumulated tens of billions in assets since launchi...

evidences.title: A Closer Look at Bitcoin's Volatility - Fidelity Digital Assets
evidences.content: \Realized volatility is calculated based on daily log returns multiplied with a factor of square root 365 to yield annualized daily realized volatility over a rolling window of 365 days. (Glassnode) Bitcoin has historically exhibited high volatility or high measures of standard deviation, but when examining its returns, many are disproportionately skewed to the positive side. This is evident in bitcoin’s Sharpe ratio of 0.96 from 2020 to early 2024—meaning while the “risk” in terms of standard deviation is higher, investors have been more than compensated for taking that risk (compared to the S&P 500’s Sharpe ratio of 0.65 over the same period). [...] Although past performance is no guarantee of future results, investors have historically experienced large price increases in short periods of time once all-time highs in price were revisited and subsequently broken under these circumstances: After a doubling in price, bitcoin historically has continued this run higher until realized volatility rises to a level where bitcoin price is becoming overheated. This is where the price appreciation phase comes to an end and seller energy looks to find its bottom. ## Conclusion Bitcoin has...

evidences.title: FOMC minutes and CPI: the macro week that sets the table
evidences.content: ## Closing context This is a week where macro and crypto-specific catalysts overlap directly. FOMC minutes and CPI arrive before the major bank earnings, meaning traders are likely to walk into Q1 results already processing fresh signals on rates and inflation. Add the CME options expiry and the regulatory backdrop of the CLARITY Act, and this is a week that rewards structured thinking over reactive positioning. Know what’s scheduled, know what’s in scope, and use the tools available to you before events land. This content is for informational purposes only and does not constitute financial advice. Past market behavior is not a reliable indicator of future results. Trading involves risk. ### Share this: ### Related Posts ## XION is available for trading! [...] For crypto-specific context: JPMorgan disclosed a Coinbase partnership last year, and Goldman’s asset management division has been active in digital asset product development. The tone of both calls, particularly any guidance on institutional appetite for risk assets, is worth monitoring alongside the macro data from earlier in the week. Relevant markets: BTC/USD, ETH/USD, spot and futures. ## BTC/ETH CME monthly options e...

evidences.title: The risk–return trade-off of Bitcoin: Evidence from regime-switching ...
evidences.content: Our goal in analyzing these return series is to uncover the time-series risk–return trade-off of Bitcoin. The sample period for our three types of Wednesday weekly returns ranges from July 21, 2010, to October 30, 2024, totaling 744 observations. Similarly, the sample periods for our three kinds of weekly returns for Monday, Tuesday, Thursday, and Friday span from July 19, 2010, to November 4, 2024 (744 observations); July 20, 2010, to October 29, 2024 (744 observations); July 22, 2010, to October 31, 2024 (743 observations); and July 23, 2010, to November 1, 2024 (744 observations), respectively. [...] Next, Panel B of Table 2 indicates that the risk parameter of Bitcoin in the high volatility state (\(\mu \_2\)) is statistically significant, with \(\mu \_1 < \mu \_2\). Moreover, the intercept of the return equation in the MS–GARCH model (6) for the high volatility state (\(\tau \_2\)) is statistically significant, with \(\tau \_1 < \tau \_2\). These findings suggest that higher Bitcoin volatility is linked to higher returns, also indicating a clear risk–return trade-off for Bitcoin’s weekly log returns on Wednesdays in the MS–GARCH model (6). Fig. 3 Estimated regime probabilit...

evidences.title: The volatility of Bitcoin and its role as a medium of exchange ... - PMC
evidences.content: Table2 presents descriptive statistics for returns and volatility. As can be seen, the average return of Bitcoin is similar across five out of the six markets. The slightly negative return observed on BTCBOX is due to the fact that the time series for this market only starts in January 2018, amidst the downturn period after the all-time high in December 2017. The minimum values, however, are similar across all markets, reflecting the sharp downturn in March 2020. As regards volatility, it is similar across all markets and suggests a daily average volatility of 3%. However, there are extremely volatile days with the maximum ranging in general around 30% where BTCBOX is again an extreme with more than 120% on a single day. In contrast, the FX rates are rather stable across the sample period [...] where $h_{t}$ ($l_{t}$) denotes the log of the highest (lowest) price on day _t_, and $o_{t}$ and $c_{t}$ are the first and the last recorded price on day _t_.5 For the subsequent analysis, we use volatility $\sigma_{t}$ obtained by taking the square root of $\sigma_{t}^{2}$. Figure2 presents time series plots of the so obtained volatility estimate. It is immediately apparent that Bitcoin...

evidences.title: Kline Candlestick Data | Binance Open Platform
evidences.content: Binance Logo Binance Logo # Kline/Candlestick Data ## API Description​ Kline/candlestick bars for a symbol. Klines are uniquely identified by their open time. Kline/Candlestick chart intervals: m -> minutes; h -> hours; d -> days; w -> weeks; M -> months ## HTTP Request​ GET `/dapi/v1/klines` `/dapi/v1/klines` ## Request Weight​ based on parameter `LIMIT` `LIMIT` | LIMIT | weight | --- | | [1,100) | 1 | | [100, 500) | 2 | | [500, 1000] | 5 | 1000 | 10 ## Request Parameters​ | Name | Type | Mandatory | Description | --- --- | | symbol | STRING | YES | After CM migration, accepts both CM and UM symbols. | | interval | ENUM | YES | | | startTime | LONG | NO | | | endTime | LONG | NO | | | limit | INT | NO | Default 500; max 1500. | [...] `startTime` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `limit` `endTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` `startTime` `endTime` `endTime` `endTime` `startTime` ## Response Example​ `[ [ 1591258320000, // Open time "9640.7", // Open "9642.4", // High "9640.6", // Low "9642.0", // Close (or latest price) "206", // Volume 1591258379999, // Close time "2.13660389", // Base asset volume 48, // Number of trades "119", // Taker buy volume "1.23424865", //...

evidences.title: Historical Market Data - Binance.US
evidences.content: Data Products Open API ## Frequently Asked Questions Candlestick Also known as k-line, is distinguished by its open time and supports multiple intervals for analysis and interpretation. Download Field Name Description open_time Candlestick open time in Unix time format open Open price high High price low Low price close Close price volume Volume close_time Candlestick close time in Unix time format quote_volume Quote asset volume count Number of trades taker_buy_base_volume Taker buy base asset volume during this period taker_buy_quote_volume Taker buy quote asset volume during this period Trades Executed transactions updated at tick level throughout daily or monthly intervals. Download Field Name Description id Trade ID price Price qty [...] All use of Historical Market Data is subject toBinance.US’ Terms of Use. evidences.title: FOMC Meeting October 2025: Fed Rate Cut and Crypto ...
evidences.content: The crypto market is at a crossroads as global investors turn their attention to the U.S. Federal Reserve’s October 28-29, 2025, FOMC meeting. The Fed’s decision on whether to lower interest rates could have major implications for crypto markets. With Bitcoin holding firm above $110,000 and DeFi platforms showing early signs of renewed activity, the stage is set for either a breakout or a reset depending on the Fed’s tone.
